family_context.preferred_cover_geography: Georgia and Germany, ideally worldwide excluding the US

purpose: Defines the discovery-stage evidence-ledger contract for candidate insurer/plan identity, source metadata, evidence classification, and cross-artifact traceability.
notes: Discovery-only artifact: this model supports candidate identification and evidence capture, not final ranking or recommendation.
The schema prefers explicit uncertainty states over omission. Unknown, inferred, gated, and not-verified states are valid when source-backed.
Null URL slots mean not verified publicly in this run, not necessarily unavailable.
A candidate record remains valid only when a stable candidate_id and at least one source-backed reference are preserved.

record_identity_contract.record_granularity: one record per discovered insurer/plan candidate relevant enough to retain in discovery, including excluded candidates when exclusion itself is source-backed and auditable
uniqueness_requirements: candidate_id must be unique within the dataset
candidate_id must be stable across derivative ledgers
source_id must be unique within candidate source_set arrays
ledger reference objects must resolve back to an existing dataset path plus candidate key or record locator

required_field_groups.description: Fields that establish the insurer/plan candidate as a stable, machine-joinable discovery record.

traceability_requirements: Must be reused unchanged across all linked discovery ledgers.
Must support joins from source, pricing, access, eligibility, and coverage ledgers.
Should reflect the insurer name shown in the supporting public source.
If insurer branding differs across sources, normalization should preserve one canonical ledger form and leave source-level variants in source_title/evidence_excerpt.

fields.allowed_values: free text normalized plan or plan-family label
fields.example: International Health Insurance / Global Medical Cover
traceability_requirements: Must preserve the discovered plan label or plan-family wording used in supporting evidence where available.
If plan name is ambiguous or product-family level only, preserve that ambiguity explicitly rather than fabricating a narrower plan name.
Classification must remain consistent with the candidate's origin in the discovery pipeline.
If a supplemental candidate later becomes primary in a future run, the change should be explicit and versioned rather than silently overwritten.

required_field_groups.description: Fields that classify whether the candidate remains relevant and how complete or visible the discovery evidence is.

traceability_requirements: Must be paired with relevance_status_reason.
Excluded candidates should still retain source-backed rationale rather than disappearing from the discovery trail.
Must summarize why the relevance_status was assigned using evidence captured elsewhere in the record or linked ledgers.

fields.allowed_values: one or more explicit uncertainty statements
traceability_requirements: Must record unresolved questions, gating, or family-fit uncertainty explicitly.
Should align with linked ambiguity, access, pricing, or coverage evidence when those artifacts exist.
Must be paired with pricing_availability_status_reason and pricing_evidence_ref.
Status must reflect reviewed public evidence rather than assumption.
Should explain whether numeric pricing was visible, partial, gated, or absent and why.
If present, this should normalize the main observed pricing-friction mode for the candidate.
Should be consistent with pricing_availability_status and supporting pricing evidence.

required_field_groups.description: Fields that identify where evidence came from, what kind of source it was, and how directly it supports the claim.

fields.allowed_values: non-empty array of source reference objects
traceability_requirements: At least one source-backed reference is mandatory per candidate entry.
Each source_ref should preserve dataset_path, dataset_section, record_bucket, source_url, source_title, and evidence_excerpt when available.
Must point to the originating machine-readable artifact that produced the source reference.
Must identify the section within the originating dataset.
Must identify the list/record container used in the source dataset.
If non-null, must be the public URL reviewed in this run.
If null, the absence must be explainable elsewhere rather than silent.
Should preserve the reviewed page/PDF title or a faithful descriptive label.
Used when the reviewed source also exposes a quote/application path relevant to discovery evidence.

allowed_values: official insurer page via search result
traceability_requirements: Normalized ledger artifacts should prefer these controlled values for evidence classification.
Raw staging artifacts may contain noisier upstream source-kind strings, but derivative ledgers should normalize them.
Must preserve the minimal source-backed excerpt or paraphrase used to justify the candidate/source link.
Should be specific enough for a reviewer to understand why the source matters without reopening the URL immediately.
If present, must identify the main verified official insurer or plan page retained for the candidate source ledger.
Null means not verified publicly in this run, not necessarily absent in reality.
If present, must be a verified public quote, application, consultation, or buy path.
Should be captured only when a broker/intermediary page materially supplements public discovery evidence.
If present, must identify a reviewed public policy wording, brochure, handbook, benefit table, or similar plan document.
Should identify the best single URL to reopen first when auditing the candidate evidence entry.

required_field_groups.description: Fields that classify evidence by role, directness, completeness, and country/coverage dimension.

traceability_requirements: Every normalized source entry must preserve whether it came directly from shared_candidate_staging or was derived later into the candidate source ledger.
Each applicable source should declare why it belongs in the candidate evidence entry instead of forcing role inference downstream.
Must be paired with source_directness_justification.
Should follow the controlled rubric in candidate_source_ledger.discovery.json and source_completeness_criteria.discovery.json.
Must explain why the source is classified as high, medium, or low directness.
Normalized candidate-source ledgers should preserve the human-facing completeness label.
When present, classification_source_value should preserve the stricter rubric value from source_completeness_criteria.
Must link the human-facing completeness label to the stricter underlying rubric.
Must be dimension-appropriate: Georgia/Germany use country assessment states; worldwide_ex_us uses worldwide status states.
Must be paired with supporting_source_refs and source_ledger_ref.
Should preserve why a dimension remains partial or ambiguous instead of collapsing everything into generic uncertainty.

fields.allowed_values: evidence-row linkage object attached to each claim-bearing nested evidence item
traceability_requirements: Every nested evidence row in derivative ledgers should explicitly identify the relevant candidate record instead of relying only on parent-record context.
At minimum, the linkage object should preserve candidate_ids, primary_candidate_id, linkage_basis, and the dataset path/section of the parent candidate record.

required_field_groups.description: Fields that link a candidate evidence entry to other machine-readable ledgers created during discovery.

traceability_requirements: Must identify the pricing evidence ledger dataset path plus a candidate-level locator.
Legacy status values may be retained for backward compatibility, but the canonical join is the dataset path plus candidate key.
Must preserve the access/intake ledger dataset path and candidate-specific evidence IDs for access and intake friction.
Must preserve the ambiguity ledger dataset path plus the candidate-specific join key.
If present, must identify the underwriting/pre-existing evidence ledger and record locator used for the summary.
Must point to the dimension-specific coverage ledger for Georgia, Germany, or worldwide_ex_us.
Every source_id listed here must resolve to a source_set entry within the same candidate record.
This map should support audit of which exact sources back which evidence roles.

country_coverage_assessment_states.confirmed: Captured public source language directly names the country or equivalent selector evidence.
country_coverage_assessment_states.inferred: Captured public source language is source-backed but indirect, such as region/area-of-cover wording or destination logic.
country_coverage_assessment_states.unknown: Reviewed sources were too generic, gated, or incomplete to support a responsible country-level inference.

worldwide_ex_us_status_states.explicitly_indicated: A reviewed public source explicitly described worldwide excluding U.S. coverage or materially equivalent wording.
worldwide_ex_us_status_states.indirectly_indicated: A reviewed public source strongly suggested a non-U.S. global configuration without explicit exact wording for the captured plan text.
worldwide_ex_us_status_states.worldwide_positioned_but_ex_us_not_verif: The plan is globally positioned, but the reviewed evidence did not directly verify an ex-U.S. configuration.
worldwide_ex_us_status_states.not_verified_from_reviewed_sources: Reviewed sources did not verify enough global geography detail to support an ex-U.S. inference.

source_directness_rubric.high: Official insurer-controlled quote/application paths and official policy wording, brochure, benefit-table, or similar plan documents.
source_directness_rubric.medium: Official insurer-controlled landing or marketing pages that are direct but less authoritative for exact eligibility or benefit details than quote or policy documents.
source_directness_rubric.low: Broker/intermediary pages or other indirect public sources that materially help discovery but are not primary insurer-controlled evidence.

url_capture_state.verified_public_url: A public URL was verified and stored in the ledger.
url_capture_state.not_verified_publicly_in_this_run: Represented as null; does not mean impossible or unavailable outside this run.

traceability_rules: Every candidate evidence entry must preserve a stable candidate_id and at least one source-backed source_ref.

Every classification claim that can affect later filtering or recommendation work should be backed either by an inline supporting_source_refs array or by a ledger reference object that resolves to another dataset.
Source normalization is allowed, but the normalized ledger must preserve both source_origin and enough original metadata to reconstruct provenance.

Derived/synthetic source rows are allowed for verified quote/application paths or plan-document URLs captured outside the original staging source_refs array, but they must be labeled as derived_candidate_source_ledger_reference.
Human-readable summaries should not be the only place a join key appears; machine-readable references must exist in JSON.
Uncertainty must be explicit. Missing evidence should be represented by controlled states, null URL capture semantics, or unresolved notes rather than silent omission.

format_selection_reason: Evidence-ledger entries contain nested citations, cross-ledger reference objects, and heterogeneous per-ledger structures that are preserved cleanly in JSONL.
JSONL supports one-record-per-evidence-entry serialization without lossy flattening.
CSV is still acceptable as a derived flat export, but not as the canonical evidence-ledger representation.

record_granularity_rules.same_candidate_multiple_findings: emit multiple records with distinct evidence_id values

key_naming_conventions.boolean_style: prefer native booleans with names that read clearly, such as is_*, has_*, was_*, or existing observed yes/no semantic fields already established in the ledger

citations_field_rule.may_be_empty_only_when: the record instead carries a non-empty supporting_source_refs array and the omission is intentional and documented by that ledger design

required_when_applicable: dataset_path or ledger_dataset_path inside cross-ledger reference objects
citation_id, source_url or null, source_type, and excerpt inside each citation object

null_and_empty_handling.null: use for expected scalar values that were not publicly verified in this run
null_and_empty_handling.empty_string: not allowed as a stand-in for unknown, gated, unavailable, or not captured
null_and_empty_handling.empty_array: allowed only when the field is structurally present and the correct meaning is no items observed or captured
null_and_empty_handling.empty_object: allowed only when the object is structurally required but currently has no populated child members; otherwise omit optional objects
null_and_empty_handling.null_semantics: null means not verified or not publicly captured in this run, not proven absent in reality
null_and_empty_handling.gating_rule: if a value is unknown because of gating or incomplete review, preserve that meaning in a paired uncertainty note or controlled status field rather than relying on null alone

wrapped_json_rules: Top-level metadata lives at the dataset root.
Array order may preserve discovery order, but joins must use stable IDs rather than position.
jsonl_rules: Each line must be a standalone valid JSON object encoded as UTF-8.
Repeat the minimal routing metadata on every line: dataset, run_scope, section, ledger_name, record_type, candidate_id, evidence_id.
Do not rely on file-level headers or sidecar metadata to interpret an individual record.
Text values containing newlines must escape them inside the JSON string, not create physical multi-line records.
csv_fallback_rules: CSV is derivative only, not canonical, for evidence ledgers.
One CSV row still equals one evidence entry.
Flatten only stable scalar fields and counts.
Nested arrays or objects should be omitted or intentionally serialized into documented JSON-string columns.
Every CSV export should preserve a path back to the canonical JSON or JSONL source via candidate_id plus evidence_id.

minimum_jsonl_template.plan_name: International Health Insurance / Global Medical Cover
minimum_jsonl_template.finding_statement: Quote page immediately asks for country of residence before continuing.

citations.excerpt: Where will you be living for the duration of the policy?*

uncertainty_notes: Later steps may add account or eligibility gating that was not verified in this run.

validation_expectations: Every JSONL line must parse as a JSON object.
No duplicate evidence_id values may exist for the same ledger_name plus candidate_id combination.
Required keys must be present even when some values are null or arrays are empty by rule.
Unknowns must be explicit through controlled states, null semantics, or uncertainty notes rather than silent omission.
canonical_schema_usage: Use this schema to validate each JSONL line independently.

For wrapped .discovery.json ledgers, validate each object inside records against the same schema after projecting dataset root metadata onto the record or when those fields are repeated at record level.
Ledger-specific fields may extend the base schema because additionalProperties remains enabled; the schema constrains the canonical minimum contract rather than every possible ledger-specific field.

canonical_schema_examples.description: High-directness access/intake evidence entry with citation-backed finding_statement.
Medium-directness pricing evidence entry using supporting_source_refs instead of citations.

canonical_validation_constraints: candidate_id must be snake_case and evidence_id must be a non-empty joinable identifier
source_origin must be one of the controlled provenance enums
source_directness_classification must be high, medium, or low
At least one of finding_statement, evidence_excerpt, or pricing_summary must be present
At least one of citations or supporting_source_refs must be non-empty

canonical_schema_artifact.applies_to: one evidence-entry record in canonical JSONL form and one record object inside wrapped JSON ledger datasets
validation_focus: controlled enums for evidence-origin and directness
at least one primary evidence text field
at least one citation or supporting_source_ref
examples for access/intake and pricing evidence entries
